c.d: The range of devices and operating systems the app supports, including mobile (iOS, Android), desktop (Windows, macOS), and web browsers.
c.s: Official app websites, app store listings

c.d: Availability of features that allow content creators to earn revenue, such as ad integration, subscriptions, donations, or virtual gifts.
Features that facilitate interaction with viewers, including live chat, polls, Q&A sessions, and interactive overlays.
The maximum supported video resolution and frame rate, indicating the quality of live streams.
Ability to stream simultaneously to multiple platforms or integrate with social media for broader reach.
User-friendliness of the interface and the simplicity of the setup process for new users.
Pricing structure, including availability of free tiers, subscription plans, and any additional fees.
c.s: Official app websites, app store listings

c.d: Tools available to manage and moderate user-generated content to ensure community guidelines are upheld.
Availability of detailed analytics and performance metrics to help creators understand their audience and improve content.
Extent to which users can personalize their streaming experience with overlays, themes, and branding elements.
